Title: Precision transcriptomics of viral foci reveals the spatial regulation of immune-signaling genes and identifies RBOHD as an important player in the incompatible interaction between potato virus Y and potato

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog; plant NADPH oxidase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and spatial restriction of virus spread during immunity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: lesion border and surrounding infected leaf tissue
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RBOHD is required for effective restriction of PVY spread and for proper spatial accumulation of salicylic acid around lesions; silencing increases viral multiplication and systemic spread
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RBOHD expression is specifically induced at the border of viral lesions during incompatible potato–PVY interaction. 2. In SA-deficient NahG plants, RBOHD expression is strongly reduced, showing SA-dependent transcriptional regulation. 3. Silencing RBOHD compromises resistance, increases lesion number and viral RNA accumulation, and permits systemic virus spread. 4. RBOHD is not required for total SA induction within lesions but is required for the tight spatial distribution of SA in surrounding tissue. 5. RNA-seq identified deregulation of UGT76D1 in shRBOHD plants, supporting a feedback link between RBOHD and SA spatial control.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial qPCR expression profiling; short-hairpin RNA silencing in transgenic potato; viral RNA quantification by qPCR; lesion counting; SA quantification by GC-MS; RNA-seq; DAB H2O2 staining
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in infected leaves, peaking at the lesion border during early visible to fully developed lesions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongest near the border of viral lesions; nearly absent in SA-depleted NahG plants after PVY inoculation
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: UGT76D1 (functional feedback-associated gene); SA signaling module
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PVY infection (PVYN-Wilga; PVYNTN; PVYN-GFP); SA depletion in NahG background
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbed RBOHD function leads to failure of virus restriction and altered SA spatial regulation; broader ROS-associated tissue response
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper treats RBOHD as a signaling/output component rather than a canonical metabolic pathway enzyme or transcription factor; its role is causal for spatial hormone regulation and immune containment. shRNA construct targeted two highly similar RBOHD loci.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving virus resistance in potato, but deployment should preserve spatial immune signaling balance and avoid compromising controlled ROS/SA patterning.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with resistance to potato virus Y
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: lesion and surrounding tissue in infected leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UGT76D1 is spatially deregulated in RBOHD-silenced plants and is implicated as a component of feedback activation controlling SA accumulation pattern
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Spatial RNA-seq identified UGT76D1 as strongly deregulated in shRBOHD plants. 2. UGT76D1 is induced in tissue surrounding lesions when RBOHD is silenced. 3. The gene is proposed to underlie altered SA accumulation outside lesions and to participate in a feedback activation loop of SA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial RNA-seq; comparative analysis of NT versus shRBOHD and NahG tissues; inference supported by SA measurements
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in section B surrounding lesions in shRBOHD plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in tissue adjacent to lesions in shRBOHD than in NT plants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PVYN-Wilga infection; RBOHD silencing; SA-deficient NahG background
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies UGT76D1 as a novel feedback-associated component of SA spatial regulation, but direct transgenic functional validation in potato was not performed here.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for tuning SA spatial homeostasis during resistance, likely best considered together with RBOHD/SA signaling context.
